successCriteria.description: cancelLeave method transitions a submitted or approved leave request to cancelled, adjusts balance (decrease pendingDays if submitted, decrease usedDays if approved), creates audit log and notification, all within a single database transaction.
discardDraft method transitions a draft leave request to cancelled, creates audit log, within a transaction.

Unit tests cover all specified scenarios: cancel from submitted, cancel from approved, cancel non-existent, cancel not owned, cancel invalid statuses (draft, rejected, already cancelled); discard draft, discard non-existent, discard not owned, discard non-draft; balance recalculation helper correctly adjusts pendingDays and usedDays.
No direct database access outside repositories; all operations go through repository interfaces.
Balance recalculation helper correctly computes inclusive days between start_date and end_date and updates the leave_balances table within the transaction.

constraints: Repository pattern: All database access goes through repository interfaces.
Audit records: All state-changing operations write an audit record.
Error handling: No unhandled promise rejections; all async errors are caught and handled.
Access control: Only the owner (employeeId) can cancel or discard their own requests.
No sensitive data in logs: Never log passwords, tokens, PII, or financial data.
outOfScope: API endpoints for cancel and discard operations.
Modifications to existing modules (leave, balance, notification, audit) beyond using their repository interfaces.
Phase 10 integration (audit logging service, RBAC enforcement, read operations).
Any other leave management features (e.g., apply, approve, reject).

ambiguities.description: Status string casing: intent uses 'Submitted' and 'Approved' with capital letters, but the authoritative architecture code uses lowercase 'submitted', 'approved', etc.
options: Use lowercase as per architecture code
